headline: FTAV’s further reading
summary: Trade war; geographic inequality; Timothée Chalamet; Luigi Mangione documentaries; etc
body_text:
Elsewhere on Thursday . . .

— Escalation and reverberations in the trade war (The Polycrisis)

— The rule of law and racial difference in the British empire (Aeon)

— FTC and Illinois AG get $25mn from Grubhub to settle charges of consumer protection and competition law violations (FTC)

— On geographic inequality (Jed Kolko for Slow Boring)

— A different era of SF crime: Computer-chip theft rings (The Believer $)

— Four Luigi Mangione Documentaries Are in the Works . . . So Far (New York Magazine, see also)

— What’s Mitchell-Lama? Timothée Chalamet Kind of Explains (NYTimes $)

— How rich musicians billed American taxpayers for luxury hotels, shopping sprees, and million-dollar bonuses (BI)
